meta.title: Alexis Herrera Saucedo | Frontend Developer — Vue, React, Next.js
meta.description: Frontend developer with 4+ years in fintech, government, and e-commerce. Vue, React, Next.js, TypeScript. Scalable architecture, reusable components, and predictable state. Mexico · remote and hybrid.
meta.keywords: Alexis Herrera Saucedo, frontend developer, Vue.js, React, Next.js, TypeScript, Nuxt.js, POS, fintech, SICT, remote Mexico

job1.description:
Back office for a digital wallet platform (Vue 2 + Nuxt.js + Vuex + Vuetify).
• Built complete company and sub-company management: registration, administration, and configuration across all three admin roles.
• Implemented financial flows: movements, fund disbursements, withdrawals, and card orders, scoped by entity level.
• Developed user management: credentials, physical card assignment, card data display, and card blocking.
• Structured the project from scratch: module organization, Vuex stores, and Axios client, laying the foundation for all subsequent development.
• Applied Atomic Design to organize UI components, making them easier to reuse and maintain throughout the project.
Key Achievements
• Developed the complete frontend of the platform as the sole responsible developer, from the initial structure to modules in production.
• Proposed a project structure with Atomic Design and independent Vuex modules that made it easier to add new requirements as the platform grew.

job2.description:
POS cash management system integrated with NetSuite ERP for MERA Corporation (React + TypeScript + React Context).
• Built coupon accumulation screens: capture, validation by type (general, prepaid, expired), and connection to NetSuite sales order generation.
• Implemented the terminal batch closing flow, allowing supervisors to close operations per point of sale at end of shift.
• Implemented banking correspondent reports (Santander, Banamex, Scotiabank) and coupon annexes, broken down by payment type, currency, and shift.
Key Achievements
• Built a reusable component library that the team adopted across multiple modules, allowing the project to move faster.

job3.description:
SICT's official transport statistics system, built from scratch (Vue 3 + Nuxt 3 + Pinia + Axios). Manages historical records from 1990 to 2023 for road, rail, and federal motor transport.
• Structured the frontend from scratch: reusable component architecture, form patterns, and data display used across all modules.
• Implemented the system's main flow: bulk Excel upload, on-screen data editing and validation, submission for review, and automatic generation of downloadable reports. Also added historical statistics views by year for quick lookups without generating reports.
Key Achievements
• Built a module for admins to configure PDF report titles and headings directly from the platform, eliminating manual document editing.

job4.description:
Ecommerce and service platforms with React, Next.js (SSR), and TypeScript for multiple clients. Focus on payment integrations, CMS connections, and SEO.
• Ourmaids: fully redesigned the site for a residential and commercial cleaning company in the U.S., integrated with SquareUp for payments. Applied SSR and SEO improvements to increase search visibility.
• Codisa: redesigned an ecommerce site from scratch, integrated with Strapi CMS and Stripe, covering the full user flow: login, product catalog, product detail, and cart with payment checkout.
• Forprint: built an internal system for the finance team to review and accept or reject payments made by bank transfer.
Key Achievements
• Codisa: the client gained full control of their catalog and promotions through Strapi, without depending on the dev team for content updates.
• Ourmaids: the redesign with SSR and SEO improvements resulted in better search rankings and an increase in client acquisition.

job5.description:
Back office for the Gmar mobility app (Vue 2 + Nuxt.js + Vuex + GraphQL). Led a frontend team of three developers.
• Built the complete Gmar back office: user management, document review and approval, trip statistics, vehicles, reviews, reports, and allowed universities table.
• Implemented real-time notifications with GraphQL.
• Led two developers on the same project, distributing tasks and reviewing code.
• Designed and developed the Gmar landing page from scratch.
• Built the La Casa Gallery ecommerce from scratch with Shopify and custom elements: collections, cart, and login.
Key Achievements
• Implemented real-time notifications that on click took the admin directly to the user's document validation screen, speeding up the approval process.
• Worked on SEO and load time improvements, which showed in search performance.

technicalTests.title: Technical tests
technicalTests.subtitle: Exercises framed the same way: the problem, your role, why that stack, and what they demonstrate.

hero.intro: Frontend developer with 4+ years building web applications for fintech, government, and e-commerce. Specialized in React, Next.js, and Vue — scalable architecture, reusable components, and predictable state. Experienced with complex systems (POS, financial back offices, data platforms) that streamline operations.
hero.highlights: 4+ years · POS · SICT · Fintech · Vue · React · TypeScript

about.title: About
about.paragraph1: Frontend developer with 4+ years of experience building web applications for fintech, government, and e-commerce. Specialized in React, Next.js, and Vue, with a focus on scalable architecture, reusable components, and predictable state management.
about.paragraph2: Experienced building complex systems (POS, financial back offices, data platforms) that streamline operational workflows and reduce manual work. Main stack: Vue and React; REST/GraphQL integration, SSR/SSG, structured metadata, and performance best practices.
about.paragraph3: Open to remote and hybrid opportunities from Mexico. I collaborate with backend and product to ship business-aligned, production-ready UI.
about.paragraph4: Education: B.Eng. Computer Systems Engineering — National Polytechnic Institute (IPN), Zacatecas, 2017–2022.

projects.title: Projects
projects.narrativeIntro: For each project: a bit of context, what I contributed, and the stack — enough detail to be useful, without a wall of text.

codisa.title: CODISA E-Commerce
codisa.technologies: Next.js · React · Strapi CMS · Stripe · TypeScript
codisa.description: Ecommerce rebuilt from scratch with Next.js and React, wired to Strapi as the CMS and Stripe for online payments. I covered the full flow: catalog, product page, cart, and checkout. The goal was for marketing and ops to update catalog and promos from Strapi without a dev deploy for every small change.

ourmaids.title: OurMaids
ourmaids.description: New marketing site for a U.S. residential and commercial cleaning company. I built the frontend with Next.js, integrated SquareUp for payments, and strengthened SSR and metadata so the offer is clearer and easier to find in search. The focus was a straightforward message and a short path to booking.

forprint.title: Forprint Backoffice
forprint.description: Internal tool so finance can review incoming bank transfers and approve or decline from one screen instead of chasing spreadsheets and threads. Built with Nuxt, Vuetify, and Pinia, with tables and filters meant for repeated daily use.

gmarLanding.title: Give Me A Ride Landing Page
gmarLanding.description: Public landing for Gmar, the mobility app — I designed and built it with Nuxt and Vuetify, keeping mobile readability in mind and structuring plans, FAQs, and legal copy. It aligns with the rest of the product and supports the brand story.

gmarBackoffice.title: Give Me A Ride Backoffice
gmarBackoffice.description: Operations back office for Gmar: users, document review, trips, vehicles, reports, and the configuration tables the business needs. Notifications are real-time over GraphQL so the team isn’t blind-refreshing; shared state across modules lives in Vuex on Nuxt.

pcppd.title: PCPPD
pcppd.description: Technical exercise focused on collaborating on GitHub with a PR-style flow. The frontend uses Vue and Vuetify with Firebase for auth and demo data so there’s no custom backend to host — it’s a compact example of how I structure team workflows.

todo.title: Todo List
todo.description: Another technical test: a todo app with Vue 3, Pinia, Vite, and Vuetify. It’s a small CRUD, but it shows state, forms, and modern tooling without leaning on outdated boilerplate.

ecommerce.title: Ecommerce
ecommerce.description: Product admin panel as a technical test, using Angular, PrimeNG, and Firebase for persistence. I leaned on form- and table-heavy patterns typical of admin UIs and kept shipping fast without writing a bespoke API within the exercise window.

lacasagallery.title: La Casa Gallery
lacasagallery.description: Online store on Shopify for an art and gift gallery. I tuned the theme and added Liquid pieces where the design needed it — featured collections, cart, and customer sign-in. Using Shopify’s hosted checkout kept payments and ops simpler than a fully custom storefront.

roadStats.title: Transport Statistics Information System (SICT)
roadStats.description: Official transport statistics system for SICT, with long historical series and bulk Excel ingestion. The Vue 3 + Nuxt 3 frontend supports on-screen validation and editing, downloadable reports, and year-level views without rebuilding PDFs by hand every cycle. Pinia and Axios keep client state and API calls aligned with the institutional workflow.

fintechBackoffice.title: Fintech Backoffice (Wallet)
fintechBackoffice.technologies: Vue 2 · Nuxt.js · Vuetify · Vuex · Axios · JavaScript
fintechBackoffice.description: Back office for a digital wallet with multiple admin levels and financial flows (movements, disbursements, cards, users). I structured the app from the ground up — clear modules, Vuex stores, and a REST client with Axios — and built the UI in Vue 2, Nuxt, and Vuetify with reusable components (Atomic Design–style) so new requirements don’t turn into a pile of one-offs.

contact.title: Contact
contact.text: Open to new opportunities and collaborations. Feel free to reach out via the form below or connect on
contact.formSuccess: Thanks! I'll get back to you soon.
contact.formError: Something went wrong. Please try again or email me directly.
